name: msteams
description: Microsoft Teams bot (Azure Bot Framework): message it in a personal chat, group chat or team channel and it answers from Elowen AI. Incoming activities arrive on the daemon webhook /hooks/msteams/messages (validated against Microsoft's JWT); replies, typing indicators and images go out through the Bot Connector REST API. Slash commands (/model, /reasoning, /display, /new, /help) with Adaptive Card pickers, AskUserQuestion as tappable cards, live tool activity, proactive cron/tick pushes and a downloadable Teams app package. Chat tools (admin sessions): conversation & member info, member lists, list conversations, plus send a message to any chat and raw connector access (operator). Each rolePolicy maps an Entra object ID / UPN / conversation ID to allowed Elowen projects + a role prompt.

configSchema.label: Connection
configSchema.hint: Azure Bot credentials. Setup: 1) Entra admin center → App registrations → New registration (single tenant) → copy the Application (client) ID and tenant ID, then Certificates & secrets → New client secret. 2) Azure portal → Create an Azure Bot bound to that app ID; set the messaging endpoint to https://<your-domain>/hooks/msteams/messages and enable the Microsoft Teams channel. 3) Upload the Teams app package to your org catalog.

configSchema.label: Microsoft App ID
configSchema.hint: The Entra app registration's Application (client) ID — also the bot ID in the Azure Bot resource.

configSchema.label: Client secret
configSchema.hint: A client secret from the app registration (Certificates & secrets). Used to call the Bot Connector API.

configSchema.label: Tenant ID
configSchema.hint: Your Entra Directory (tenant) ID — the bot authenticates against this tenant (single-tenant app registration).

configSchema.label: Notification conversation
configSchema.hint: Where proactive messages (cron/tick results) are posted — a Teams conversation ID the bot has already seen. Empty = no proactive pushes.

configSchema.label: Replies & behavior
configSchema.hint: When and how the bot answers.

configSchema.label: Respond without mention
configSchema.hint: On: in group chats the bot answers every message from a mapped sender. Off: it only answers when @mentioned. Team channel posts always require an @mention — Teams only delivers those to the bot.

configSchema.label: Tool activity
configSchema.hint: What the progress message shows while the agent works: nothing, tool status lines, or status plus live output tails.

configSchema.label: Answer delivery
configSchema.hint: Final posts one reply when the turn ends; live streams the answer into a message as it is written.

configSchema.label: Tool output detail
configSchema.hint: How much of each tool's result the progress message keeps once the tool finishes.

configSchema.label: Tool message layout
configSchema.hint: Single keeps one edited progress message; per-tool posts a message per tool call.

configSchema.label: Runtime footer
configSchema.hint: Append a small "model · context %" line under the final reply.

configSchema.label: Show reasoning
configSchema.hint: Stream the model's reasoning into the progress message (extended-thinking models). Off by default — it's usually noise.

configSchema.label: Service language
configSchema.hint: Language of the bot's own service messages (errors, command replies). The agent itself answers in the user's language.

configSchema.label: Conversation
configSchema.hint: Image routing.

configSchema.label: Vision model
configSchema.hint: Model used for turns that carry image attachments (must support vision). Empty = the chat's normal model.

configSchema.label: Media limits
configSchema.hint: Caps on inbound/outbound images and how long a pending question stays open.

configSchema.label: Max inbound image size
configSchema.hint: Largest image attachment (bytes) the bot downloads for vision.

configSchema.label: Max images per message
configSchema.hint: How many image attachments in a single incoming message the bot sends to the vision model.

configSchema.label: Max images per reply
configSchema.hint: How many generated images the bot attaches to a single outgoing reply.

configSchema.label: Question timeout (ms)
configSchema.hint: How long a pending AskUserQuestion card stays answerable.

configSchema.label: Role policies
configSchema.hint: Map Teams senders to allowed projects, a role prompt and the tools the bot may use.

configSchema.label: Role policies
configSchema.hint: First matching policy wins; senders with no mapped policy are ignored. The ID is the sender's Entra object ID (a GUID), their UPN/email, or a whole conversation ID (grants everyone in that chat).
